_brokerHint.text: This adapter runs its own MQTT broker. Point the Lithernet Casambi gateway's MQTT client at this host and port. SSL is not supported by the gateway - use a trusted network.

gatewayId.label: Gateway ID (topic segment)
gatewayId.help: The <deviceId> set in the gateway web UI; topics are casambi/<id>/...

buttonCount.label: Injectable buttons
buttonCount.help: Number of virtual buttons to create for injecting press/release events into Casambi (0 = none)

removeOrphans.label: Remove orphaned objects
removeOrphans.help: After a full poll cycle on start, delete scene/group/device objects that no longer exist on the gateway (e.g. a scene deleted in the Casambi app). Off = orphans are kept.

orphanScanDelay.label: Cleanup scan delay (s)
orphanScanDelay.help: How long to collect feedback before the cleanup runs - must exceed one full poll cycle so nothing real is missed.

_namesHint.text: Assign friendly names to scenes, groups and devices by their number (as shown in the Casambi app). Names are applied as the channel name in objects, e.g. devices.12 -> "Kitchen Spot". Leave empty to keep the number.

_cloudHint.text: The Casambi cloud is the source of truth for device/scene structure, names and capabilities (own network credentials, no developer API key). It is required - it provides the catalog; live state and control come from the MQTT gateway (optional) on top. Control needs one single-member scene per device in the Casambi app.

cloudUuid.label: Network UUID
cloudUuid.help: Casambi app: More -> Network Setup -> iBeacon -> UUID.

cloudPassword.label: Network password
cloudPassword.help: The network (admin) password set when the network was created or shared. Stored encrypted.

cloudSyncInterval.label: Auto-sync every (minutes)
cloudSyncInterval.help: Re-read the cloud catalog on this interval (0 = only at start / on demand). Trigger an immediate sync via the state control.syncNow; the last run is shown in info.lastSync.

cloudDeviceRange.label: Build device range
cloudDeviceRange.help: Which device IDs to build objects for. "0-*" = all, e.g. "1-30" or "5". Useful to try a subset.

cloudSceneRange.label: Build scene range
cloudSceneRange.help: Which scene IDs to build objects for. "0-*" = all, e.g. "1-80".
